system_prompt:
You are an expert VLSI Verification Engineer. Your task is to perform Fault Localization and RTL Repair. Analyze the provided Module Description, Error Symptom, dynamic VCD Trace, and Sliced Verilog code. 
You must output a JSON object with exactly three keys:
1. "buggy_line": The exact integer line number from the Sliced Verilog where the defect exists.
2. "root_cause": A 1-2 sentence explanation of why the logic failed at the failing timestamp.
3. "fixed_code": The corrected single line (or block) of Verilog code.
user_prompt:
### [Module Description]
MODULE SPECIFICATION: design_4 (Arbiter)
1. ARCHITECTURAL INTENT

    Type: 4-Channel Arbiter.

    Intended Scheme: Round-Robin priority with a sticky bus lock.

    Mechanism: Grants access to a single shared resource among four competing requesters (req0 to req3). It is designed to use a 2-bit priority mask (lmask) to remember the last granted agent and rotate priority, preventing starvation.

2. PORT INTERFACE

    Global: * clk: System Clock.

        rst: Active-high synchronous reset. Clears all active grants and resets the priority mask to 0.

    Inputs: * req3, req2, req1, req0: Active-high request lines. Upon reset, req0 holds the default highest priority.

    Outputs: * gnt3, gnt2, gnt1, gnt0: One-hot active-high grant signals indicating which agent holds the resource.

3. CRITICAL INTERNAL STATE (Registers & Wires)

    lgnt0 - lgnt3 (Latched Grants): Registered, one-hot grant outputs that maintain the current arbitration state.

    lcomreq / comreq (Bus Lock): Evaluates to 1 if any currently granted agent is still asserting its request. Acts as a sticky lock; while high, no new arbitrations can occur, preventing request hijacking.

    lmask1, lmask0 (Priority Mask): State registers intended to store the encoded ID of the last granted agent. The combinational Boolean logic uses this mask to dynamically shift priority to the next agent in the ring.

    mask_enable: Control register intended to trigger the state update of the lmask registers.

    beg (Begin Signal): Evaluates to 1 when there is at least one active request AND the bus is currently free (~lcomreq).

4. BEHAVIORAL CONSTRAINTS & DEBUGGING CONTEXT

    Priority Degradation: If the priority mask (lmask) fails to update (e.g., due to an undriven or floating mask_enable signal), the complex Boolean equations will statically default to a strict Fixed-Priority scheme where req3 > req2 > req1 > req0.

    Sticky Grants: A grant must remain asserted continuously as long as its corresponding request line remains high and the system is not reset.

### [Error Symptom]
=================================================================
TEST FAILED AT 80.0 ns
FAILING SIGNAL : gnt1
ERROR DETAILS  : Expected gnt1 to be 1, got 0
=================================================================

  33: reg             lgnt2     ;
  34: reg             lgnt3     ;

  59:

  61:         | (~lcomreq & ~lmask1 &  lmask0 & ~req3 & ~req2 &  req1 & ~req0)
  62:         | (~lcomreq &  lmask1 & ~lmask0 & ~req3 &  req1 & ~req0)
  63:         | (~lcomreq &  lmask1 &  lmask0 &  req1 & ~req0)
  64:         | ( lcomreq &  lgnt1);
  65:       
  66:   lgnt2 <=(~lcomreq & ~lmask1 & ~lmask0 &  req2  & ~req1)
  67:         | (~lcomreq & ~lmask1 &  lmask0 &  req2)
  68:         | (~lcomreq &  lmask1 & ~lmask0 & ~req3 &  req2  & ~req1 & ~req0)
  69:         | (~lcomreq &  lmask1 &  lmask0 &  req2 & ~req1 & ~req0)
  70:         | ( lcomreq &  lgnt2);
  71:       
  72:   lgnt3 <=(~lcomreq & ~lmask1 & ~lmask0 & req3  & ~req2 & ~req1)
  73:         | (~lcomreq & ~lmask1 &  lmask0 & req3  & ~req2)
  74:         | (~lcomreq &  lmask1 & ~lmask0 & req3)
  75:         | (~lcomreq &  lmask1 &  lmask0 & req3  & ~req2 & ~req1 & ~req0)
  76:         | ( lcomreq & lgnt3);
  77: end 
...
  86: assign lcomreq = ( req3 & lgnt3 )
  87:                 | ( req2 & lgnt2 )
  88:                 | ( req1 & lgnt1 )
  89:                 | ( req0 & lgnt0 );
...
  94: assign  lgnt =  {(lgnt3 | lgnt2),(lgnt3 | lgnt1)};
...

 100: if( rst )

 103:     lmask0 <= 0;

 113:       lmask0 <= lmask0;

...
